topic: A person using a 'Self-Checkout' machine at a grocery store.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Front view: A person uses the self-checkout machine to scan an item.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Close-up: The hands press the payment button on the self-checkout screen.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Wide: The person bags the scanned items and retrieves the receipt.
parsed_generation.narration: The shopper approaches the self-checkout, scans the item, confirms payment, and bags the goods. The sequence unfolds with crisp, focused motion across eight seconds.

feedback: The output stays on-topic and uses timestamped, camera-angle shots with concise, action-first language, matching the structure style. However, it omits several sensory and procedural details from the gold standard (beep, price display/update, and receipt printing). Adding a beat that shows the price update and the receipt printing would bring it closer to the gold standard and enhance cinematic depth.
strengths: Clear three-shot sequence with distinct camera angles (front, close-up, wide).
On-topic focus on the self-checkout interaction and payment flow.
fixes: Include the beeping sound cue, price display on screen, and a receipt-print moment to mirror the gold standard.
Consider aligning shot timing to emphasize the price/tap-then-pay sequence and add a moment of the receipt beginning to print for added cinematic depth.
